unit.name: Recap: image classification, kNN, linear scores, and data loss
unit.description: Connects image classification and linear classifiers to the loss formulation used later for regularization and optimization.
unit.summary: Image classification is framed as mapping an image to one label from a fixed set such as cat, dog, bird, deer, or truck [ts=27s]. The recap emphasizes the semantic gap between human perception and pixel tensors, plus viewpoint, illumination, deformation, occlusion, clutter, and intraclass variation [ts=64s]. Because hand-written rules are inadequate, kNN provides a data-driven baseline: 1-NN copies the closest training label, and k-NN uses the most common label among nearby examples [ts=182s]. Validation data is used to choose hyperparameters such as k, while the test set is saved for final unseen-data evaluation [ts=216s]. Linear classifiers compute class scores by flattening a 32x32x3 image into 3,072 values and multiplying by W plus bias [ts=558s]. A loss function formalizes how bad the classifier is: dataset loss averages per-example losses, and Softmax/cross-entropy is high when the correct class has low predicted probability [ts=626s].

key_points.text: Image classification maps an input image to a label from a fixed label set.
Recognition is difficult because pixels differ from semantic human perception and because images vary by viewpoint, illumination, deformation, occlusion, clutter, and intraclass variation.
In kNN, 1-NN assigns the closest training label; larger k assigns the most common label among neighbors.
Use validation data to choose hyperparameters and reserve the test set for final unseen-data evaluation.
A 32x32 RGB image has 32*32*3 = 3,072 values for a linear classifier.
Dataset loss is the average of per-example losses.

kp_catalog.name: Image classification and recognition challenges
kp_catalog.description: Assigning an image to a label despite semantic gap, viewpoint, illumination, deformation, occlusion, clutter, and intraclass variation. The task of assigning one label from a fixed vocabulary to an input image.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because image classification and recognition challenges formalizes how assigning an image to a label despite semantic gap, viewpoint, illumination, deformation, occlusion, clutter, and intraclass variation, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context though roles split between gateway and supporting.

kp_catalog.name: Data-driven kNN and validation/test splits
kp_catalog.description: Using nearest training examples to classify and using validation data to select hyperparameters while preserving test data for final evaluation.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because data-driven kNN and validation/test splits clarifies using nearest training examples to classify and using validation data to select hyperparameters while preserving test data for final evaluation, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Linear classifier scores and loss functions
kp_catalog.description: Linear score functions, Softmax/cross-entropy, and dataset loss as an average of per-example losses.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because linear classifier scores and loss functions formalizes how linear score functions, Softmax/cross-entropy, and dataset loss as an average of per-example losses, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
